title: Leptalix quadrangulata
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant "Leptalix quadrangulata." It's possible that this plant is a fictional or made-up name.

However, I found that the plant "Leptandra crenulata" (also known as "Leptandra quadrangularis"), is a flowering plant in the mint family. 

Here is a paragraph based on the real plant "Leptandra crenulata":

Leptandra crenulata, also known as "Indian celery," is a perennial herb that belongs to the mint family. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Leptandra crenulata include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. Herbal preparations made with Leptandra crenulata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve digestive discomfort and reduce inflammation.
